stories.body: Hours after Israel accepted what U.S. officials described as a renewed ceasefire with Hezbollah, Israeli strikes killed dozens in southern Lebanon and Hezbollah claimed ambushes that left four Israeli soldiers dead, underscoring how fragile the Trump administration’s broader memorandum with Iran has become; Lebanese officials counted at least 47 fatalities around Nabatieh while Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem declared Israel’s campaign a failure, and the violence forced U.S.–Iran implementation talks in Switzerland to be scrapped even as Trump emissaries scrambled to keep diplomacy alive.
stories.headline: Israel-Hezbollah truce buckles amid airstrikes and cancelled Iran talks

stories.body: U.S. Central Command said forces shot down four Iranian drones aimed at maritime traffic near the Strait of Hormuz and then destroyed radar and surveillance installations at Goruk and Qeshm Island, an escalation that complicates already-delicate ceasefire negotiations linking Gaza, Lebanon and the U.S.–Iran war; Trump told NBC Iran retains barely a fifth of its missile arsenal while Tehran tied any regional peace to Israel’s withdrawal from southern Lebanon, where fighting between Israeli troops and Hezbollah continues despite American pressure for restraint.
stories.headline: U.S. hits Iranian radar after drone threats to Hormuz shipping

stories.body: Australia’s agriculture minister Julie Collins confirmed that a brown skua found near Esperance, Western Australia, died of H5N1 bird flu and that a second seabird is suspected of infection, ending the continent’s status as free of the highly pathogenic strain; authorities have convened emergency disease committees, stepped up wildlife surveillance and urged the public not to touch sick birds, while conservationists warned the virus could devastate native species even though there is no evidence yet of poultry outbreaks.
stories.headline: Australia detects first H5N1 case as virus reaches every continent

stories.body: Delegations at the Next Steps conference in Accra endorsed a 19-point plan demanding debt relief, restitution of looted heritage and a global reparations fund following a UN resolution that labeled the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity; Ghana’s President John Dramani Mahama urged former slave-trading nations to offer unconditional apologies, while participants emphasized that reparatory justice must include structural economic remedies as well as acknowledgment of the disproportionate impact on African women and girls.
stories.headline: African and Caribbean leaders adopt global reparations framework

stories.body: Authorities across Western Europe warned of record temperatures as France saw schools adjust schedules amid forecasts of 41C highs, an unusual concentration of heat in northern and western regions, while the continent continued cleaning up from earlier severe storms that brought 65 mph winds, golf-ball hail and widespread damage from Slovenia through Greece, illustrating the volatility of Europe’s early summer weather extremes.
stories.headline: Western Europe faces searing heat after storms batter Balkans

stories.body: In a 52-47 pre-dawn vote, Senate Republicans approved a bill steering an extra $70 billion to Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol over the next three years, defeating Democratic attempts to permanently bar a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that critics call a slush fund for Trump allies; GOP leaders insisted Justice Department assurances make the fund moot even as moderate Republicans facing tight races sought amendment votes to scrap it outright before the House takes up the measure.
stories.headline: Senate advances $70 billion ICE funding while leaving Trump ‘anti-weaponization’ fund intact

stories.body: Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang struck down dozens of Missouri abortion restrictions, including mandatory dual in-person visits and physician-observed pill ingestion, finding they violate the voter-approved 2024 constitutional amendment that allows abortion up to fetal viability; Planned Parenthood said it will resume prescribing abortion pills in-state for the first time since 2018 even as the state prepares an appeal and anti-abortion groups pursue another ballot measure.
stories.headline: Missouri ruling restores medication abortions under 2024 constitutional protections

stories.body: Barack and Michelle Obama celebrated the Obama Presidential Center’s dedication on Chicago’s South Side alongside former presidents Joe Biden, George W. Bush and Bill Clinton, using the Juneteenth-timed opening to urge defense of democratic norms while showcasing exhibits and programming meant to connect local communities with global leaders, celebrities and cultural figures who attended the ceremony.
stories.headline: Obama Presidential Center opens with bipartisan show of support

stories.body: As the Supreme Court nears a ruling on President Donald Trump’s executive order targeting birthright citizenship, the ACLU warned that the administration’s constitutional arguments could jeopardize millions of Americans born on U.S. soil, spotlighting figures such as soccer star Folarin Balogun, whose World Cup success underscores the human stakes while justices signaled skepticism during arguments over whether the executive can override the Fourteenth Amendment on its own.
stories.headline: Supreme Court scrutiny grows over Trump’s bid to curb birthright citizenship

stories.body: Danco Laboratories and GenBioPro asked the Supreme Court for an emergency stay of a unanimous Fifth Circuit ruling that reinstated in-person dispensing requirements for the abortion pill mifepristone, arguing the decision creates immediate confusion for patients and providers by overriding FDA rules that allow telehealth prescriptions and mail delivery; the appeals court move represents the most significant federal restriction on medication abortion since Roe v. Wade was overturned.
stories.headline: Drugmakers seek Supreme Court relief after appellate court curbs mail-order mifepristone

stories.body: D.C. Council member Janeese Lewis George secured the Democratic mayoral nomination, effectively positioning her to succeed Mayor Muriel Bowser next year after pledging universal child care, social housing and an uncompromising stance against Trump administration efforts to federalize local police or expand cooperation with immigration agents; her victory over Kenyan McDuffie comes as Washington prepares for new leadership across City Hall and its congressional delegation, raising fears of further clashes with a White House already threatening to curtail home rule.
stories.headline: Janeese Lewis George wins D.C. mayoral primary, promises confrontation with Trump

stories.body: National Court judge José Luis Calama kept former prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero under investigation for alleged influence peddling, money laundering and other offenses tied to the Plus Ultra airline case, and at prosecutors’ request summoned his daughters Alba and Laura Rodríguez Espinosa along with longtime secretary Gertrudis Alcázar, while rejecting fresh requests for U.S. assistance on cellphone data and fueling debate over undeclared luxury jewelry linked to the former leader.
stories.headline: Judge widens Plus Ultra probe to Zapatero’s daughters and aide

stories.body: A preliminary report by the Civil Guard’s UCO unit concluded that an Alberto González Amador company lacked the staff and tools to justify lucrative consulting fees from private healthcare group Quirón in 2021, reinforcing tax agency findings that Ayuso’s partner billed €4.4 million over three years as his relationship with the Madrid premier began; the national leadership of the Popular Party dismissed the controversy as a private matter even as opposition parties cite it while pressing for Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez to call elections.
stories.headline: Civil Guard questions Ayuso partner’s Quirón consulting payments

stories.body: PSOE, Sumar and allied parties noted that Junts per Catalunya has increasingly aligned with the conservative PP—and even far-right Vox—on parliamentary votes while demanding early elections, a stance government sources link to expectations that the EU Court of Justice’s July 16 ruling on the Catalan amnesty law will determine Carles Puigdemont’s legal fate and reshape negotiating leverage in Madrid.
stories.headline: Junts’ voting shifts tied to looming EU ruling on Catalan amnesty

stories.body: During the Brussels European Council, Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen and Italy’s Giorgia Meloni challenged Pedro Sánchez over Spain’s expedited program that has already drawn 900,000 applications for legal status, arguing the move affects other member states and reiterating their push for offshore deportation centers, while Sánchez rejected externalization plans as ineffective and defended a more humanitarian strategy alongside opposition from Hungary, Belgium and others who signed a letter urging third-country return arrangements.
stories.headline: EU partners press Sánchez over Spain’s fast-track migrant regularization

stories.body: Junts per Catalunya’s roughly 1,000 Barcelona members begin online voting this weekend after the four contenders—Jordi Martí Galbis, Pilar Calvo, Glòria Freixa and Jaume Alonso-Cuevillas—held a final debate focused on housing budgets, policing strategy and bolstering Catalan identity, with candidates promising everything from taking direct control of the Guàrdia Urbana to stepping back from Madrid duties to prepare for the 2027 municipal campaign.
stories.headline: Junts members set to pick 2027 Barcelona mayoral candidate

stories.body: The heaviest section of the tunnel boring machine for prolonging Ferrocarrils de la Generalitat’s L8 line from Plaça d’Espanya to Gràcia will traverse Barcelona after midnight on 23 June, moving from the port up Paral·lel to the Gran Via launch shaft where crews have already installed the cutter head and four trailers; once assembled, the machine will excavate a 3.6 km tunnel with two new stations over about 13 months, with excavated soil conveyed via an underground gallery to a staging pit in Parc Joan Miró.
stories.headline: 300-ton tunnel boring machine to be lowered for L8 metro extension

stories.body: In a commemorative session at Parliament, President Salvador Illa and Barcelona mayor Jaume Collboni hailed the Àrea Metropolitana de Barcelona as a model of “municipal federalism” and called for stronger multi-level governance to serve the five-million-person conurbation, arguing that reinforcing the AMB’s consensual framework—born in 2010 after the dissolution of the old metropolitan corporation—helps balance capital-city leadership with support for surrounding municipalities and even offers lessons for the European Union.
stories.headline: Catalan leaders mark 15 years of the Barcelona Metropolitan Area

stories.body: Organizers of the 14–18 October Barcelona Boat Show will dedicate new space at the Moll de la Fusta, Moll d’Espanya and Marina Port Vell to scuba diving, dinghy sailing and surf, aiming to attract newcomers as well as yacht buyers by offering demonstrations on a floating platform, more sailing and catamaran models, and a large pavilion for water sports brands—an expansion that comes as Spain’s nautical industry logged a 10.6% rise in boat registrations in the first five months of 2026.
stories.headline: Barcelona Boat Show to add water sports hub
